item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image is of Oisin Bryne's Universe, with no indication it has been released by the artist under a license compliant with the wiki's (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, it has been removed.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Good suggestion, I will make that as well when I edit.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Don't forget, Wondertainment has a hub now.

I suggest changing this:

The outer packaging describes the item as Dr. Wondertainment’s Little Big-Banger

To this:

The outer packaging describes the item as Dr. Wondertainment’s Little Big-Banger

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

Reading this reminded me of two SCPs, one new and one old, both with similar subject matters. SCP-536 allows for the modification of universal constants; and SCP-3049 is a mini-Big Bang creator. As such, both can be easily crosslinked.

Editing this:

When a set of parameters is chosen on the remote control and the Start button pressed, a new universe is produced from a singularity within the SCP-1696 container, using the chosen parameters.

To this:

When a set of parameters is chosen on the remote control and the Start button pressed, a new universe is produced from a singularity within the SCP-1696 container, using the chosen parameters.1

And editing this:

Your Dr. Wondertainment’s Little Big-BangerTM allows you to change the following settings carried over from our previous

To this

Your Dr. Wondertainment’s Little Big-BangerTM allows you to change the following settings carried over from our previous

Footnotes
1. Research into the similarity of function between SCP-1696 and SCP-3049 is pending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there a limit to how many spatial dimensions one can create with 1696?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh god you just gave me an existential crisis… i do not feel comfortable now…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assume the skip the case is similar to is 699?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dissapointed in the lack of mention of the glasses outside the note, but still +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, this is one of those scps that I really, really want

I guess I could settle for an experiment log

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this skip. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. This should be elaborated on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since this is missing the recovery log, did they find the hyper zoom glasses? If yes, why aren't they explained, or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this might be my favorite Wondertainment. Educational, wonderful, funderful, and not just "kids toy but it actually does what it's supposed to."

Also, I dig that it's "YOUR universe," not "OUR universe."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, Wondertainment could also be female or genderless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love Wondertainment. He/they know how to get anyone interested in Science!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "To avoid interference with automatic sensors, do not bring magnets within 1 meter of your Little Big-BangerTM." Love that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unless the box has some nifty zoom features, it might as well be a programmable lava-lamp. If you condense the entire universe down to less than 1 meter cubed, all you get is swirls of light and color. There is zero chance of the most powerful microscopes conceivable detecting anything as piddling as a planet, much less something really cool like box-verse people.

Meh, I still like it. But, not being a physcist, I'm more interested in where I can get a license to create sentients. Is that something they do at the DMV? Or do I have to go down to the county courthouse?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that question answers itself. Especially when you consider, you know, Pandora.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why would they want to open it? God knows what crazy shit would commence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah, that hint merely got the taste of blood in my mouth!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I did sneak a hint in with "negative baryon number violation" (which, if I understand correctly, would mean a universe made mostly out of anti-matter). Either the Foundation made one and something bad happened, or having a box of anti-matter contained only by mysterious Dr. Wondertainment technology just makes everyone too nervous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what Wondertainment is all about!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is excellent, but I almost downvoted when I got to the bottom and there was no experiment log! If EVER there were an SCP that needed some! But in that you had my interest that piqued, I guess you earn an upvote!

(p.s. write some experiment logs!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this.

As others have stated, I want one too.

upvoted, of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a hell of a lot more than 536.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should I mention that the forms work despite having a nonexistent address on them?

I figured the Tech Support line worked a bit like the order line from SCP-000-J i.e. untraceable, and if you ask something other than a support question, they ignore you and eventually hang up.

Edit: I added a bit about both of those items.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perfect Wondertainment. It just looks awesome, and has the perfect "inner child says gimme, gimme, gimme reasonable mind says wait this is a very bad idea"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done. To me, 536 is the larger, experimental model, while this is the kid friendly version. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it bad that I really, really want one for myself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good job, even despite the similarities to 536.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was fun. Wondertainment is quickly becoming a favorite of mine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you decided to write this, Eric. :) +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I agree that the phone number ought to be addressed, there's already precedent for Wondertainment order forms that inexplicably work; see the chemistry set for example.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a few questions that this article seems to leave unanswered, the first being: If there is a re-order card, is there an address on it that the Foundation raided? Or does it just disappear when put in a mailbox, or what? Also what happens when you call the tech support number?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want one of these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I realize this has a somewhat similar effect as SCP-536, but think the look and feel of it are different enough. Also, even if you consider this the goofy kid's version of 536, there are some scary implications:

One of the things I am hoping people get from it is that if you use it with the settings of our own universe, there is probably a little Earth in the box, possibly with an alternate version of you. And then you're going to hit the Reset button…

Conversely, we could all be in a giant version of one of these things, and have no way of knowing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh… SCP-536 did it better. And nothing here really grabs my attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I edited that line to clarify.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has some similarities to SCP-536 (both enable the user to vary universal constants within a box), but it's different enough for me. I desperately want to know what happens if you create sentient beings without a license.

Possible word omission with "expansion of your universe will automatically stop when it reaches 5cm of the outer container:" it's unclear what the 5 cm measurement refers to.
